title: After-Action Report — retiring wERA, anchoring xRA to the run environment
scope: Scope: o27v2/web/app.py (pitching constants, aggregator, baselines), tests/test_stat_invariants.py (invariant 8), 13 templates + test_template_renders.

rationale.item: wERA weighted earned runs by arc … asserting a run allowed late is worth more than the same run early. That theory has nothing to stand on in O27. In a single continuous 27-out half, every run counts identically toward the final score. So wERA wasn't measuring run prevention; it was measuring run prevention + a wrong theory about timing.
xRA measures the thing wERA was pretending to measure, without the fallacy. It's the run-prevention companion to the RE24-native run values used for BSR and 2C Runs.

delegation_events.quote: Invariant 8 UPDATED, not deleted (per the owner's call — keep the check, point it at the surviving stat)

actions.description: Dropped the arc-weighted ER / C_w computation; werra / wera_plus keys retained as aliases of xra / xra+ (≈40 references keep working)
Anchored xRA to league RA per 27 outs; fixed anchoring bug by routing both sides through one shared _xra_run_values helper, xra_norm = total_RA / Σ(run_values_p)
pWAR rebased to xRA: VORP = (replacement_xRA − my_xRA) × outs/27, replacement = league xRA × 1.20
Invariant 8 now asserts league xRA (outs-weighted) tracks league RA/27 within 0.05, plus a guard that werra aliases xra; query gained breakdown columns
UI relabel: wERA+ → xRA+, wERA → xRA across 13 templates; arc-weighting tooltips rewritten

risk_posture: The `werra` / `wera_plus` keys are retained as **aliases of `xra` / `xra+`** so leaderboards, dispersion, standings and templates keep working with zero field-plumbing churn (≈40 references).
Left as a scoped, low-risk follow-up rather than risk a blind 13-template structural pass.

validation_claims.claim: Invariant 8 passes exactly (league xRA == league RA/27 to ~0; was failing at 0.062 for the old wERA, then 0.725 → 0.0607 → 0 as the anchoring fix landed)
validation_claims.check_method: Run tests/test_stat_invariants.py invariant 8 against a seeded DB

validation_claims.claim: Full stat-invariant suite: 11 passed. o27/tests: 106. o27v2/tests: 128 passed (the 1 fail is the pre-existing flaky GM-noise trade sampling test)
All pages render 200; no template says "wERA" anymore
validation_claims.check_method: Render all pages and grep templates for wERA

non_actions.item: Template column de-dup (some templates display the xRA value twice)
non_actions.reason: Left as a scoped, low-risk follow-up rather than risk a blind 13-template structural pass

non_actions.item: Optional descriptive arc splits (early/mid/late ER with no weighting)
non_actions.reason: Decay already carries the real fade signal. Not needed for the core move.

residual_risks: Some templates display the xRA value twice (retained werra-fed column next to the xra-fed one); labels correct.
Two pre-existing tests/test_template_renders failures (gmli field; season-archive writer) fail identically before this work — data/fixture-dependent, worth a look on a proper season-archive DB.
open_hooks: Per-template removal of the redundant xRA column.
Descriptive early/mid/late ER breakdown if 'when did the damage happen' is wanted back.
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — retiring wERA, anchoring xRA to the run environment
1. Why wERA had to go (the fallacy)
unmapped_content: wERA arc weights: outs 1–9 ×0.85, 10–18 ×1.00, 19–27 ×1.20. The real leverage of a run is situational — what WPA / leverage index capture. Anchoring bug detail: xra_norm computed from blended league hit-shares while per-pitcher xRA used the actual allowed singles/doubles/triples breakdown — method mismatch leaving league xRA ~0.5 off. Commits d8eb5aa → 6072cdc; branch claude/war-oaa-reconciliation-koalas-7t0pG.
parser_notes: Date from 'Date:' header (2026-06-06). Same branch as the WAR/OAA reconciliation AARs.
